name: Incomplete List of Disallowed Inputs
description: The product implements a protection mechanism that relies on a list of inputs (or properties of inputs) that are not allowed by policy or otherwise require other action to neutralize before additional processing takes place, but the list is incomplete.

peers.name: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')
Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')
Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
Improper Control of Filename for Include/Require Statement in PHP Program ('PHP Remote File Inclusion')

mapping.rationale: This CWE entry is at the Base level of abstraction, which is a preferred level of abstraction for mapping to the root causes of vulnerabilities.

mechanism.root_cause_summary: The product implements a protection mechanism that relies on a list of inputs (or properties of inputs) that are not allowed by policy or otherwise require other action to neutralize before additional processing takes...
typical_preconditions: untrusted or insufficiently validated state reaches sensitive operation
typical_sources: untrusted or insufficiently validated state reaches sensitive operation
negative_nonfix_signals: style refactor without changing the sensitive path

guard_patterns: same logical object and same sink context

recommended_guard_templates: same logical object and same sink context

stage1.goal: Classify patch chunks by how directly they repair the CWE root cause.
must_focus: direct change to vulnerable sink or validation site
changes that alter boundary, lifecycle, policy, or authorization relation
supporting helper or error-propagation logic needed by the main fix
must_avoid: generic logging changes without security semantics
refactors unrelated to the sensitive sink/path

stage2.goal: Construct discriminative root cause, anchor, predicates, and guards.
must_focus: anchor at the sink or dominant validation block
prefer predicates that encode relations, not lone generic tokens
choose function-local or anchor-window scope whenever possible
must_avoid: predicates that rely on generic names alone
anchors in wrappers that lack the core vulnerability mechanism
recommended_queries: same logical object and same sink context

stage3.goal: Verify whether the target tag still exhibits the vulnerable relation or already contains the fix relation.
must_focus: whether the sensitive sink/path still exists
whether the fix relation is locally present in the same code region
whether anchor relocation is required before concluding absence
must_avoid: declaring NOT_AFFECTED from generic token absence alone
using broad token matches as final evidence

bad_examples.summary: The following code attempts to stop XSS attacks by removing all occurences of "script" in an input string.
bad_examples.snippet: public String removeScriptTags(String input, String mask) { return input.replaceAll("script", mask); }

bad_examples.summary: This example takes user input, passes it through an encoding scheme, then lists the contents of the user's home directory based on the user name.
bad_examples.snippet: sub GetUntrustedInput { return($ARGV[0]); } sub encode { my($str) = @_; $str =~ s/\&/\&/gs; $str =~ s/\"/\"/gs; $str =~ s/\'/\'/gs; $str =~ s/\</\</gs; $str =~ s/\>/\>/gs; return($str); } sub doit {...

observed_examples.description: Chain: AI agent platform does not restrict pathnames containing internal "/./" sequences (CWE-55), leading to an incomplete denylist (CWE-184) that does not prevent OS command injection (CWE-78)

Chain: API for text generation using Large Language Models (LLMs) does not include the "\" Windows folder separator in its denylist (CWE-184) when attempting to prevent Local File Inclusion via path traversal (CWE-22),...
Chain: filter only checks for some shell-injection characters (CWE-184), enabling OS command injection (CWE-78)
product uses a denylist to identify potentially dangerous content, allowing attacker to bypass a warning
PHP remote file inclusion in web application that filters "http" and "https" URLs, but not "ftp".
Programming language does not filter certain shell metacharacters in Windows environment.
